extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco won the final stage after escaping with ROGLIC on the circuits, but lost GC by 6 seconds after Quick-Step's isolation tactics failed on repeated laps.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step's aggressive pace-setting on circuits shredded Evenepoel's support, forcing him to attack alone at 26km—revealing domestique depth matters more than GC strength on circuits.
UAE Emirates showed team dysfunction as Soler escaped ahead while Ayuso-Yates closed gap behind, splitting effort instead of consolidating for podium.
Bora-Hansgrohe refused to chase Evenepoel-Roglic duo despite trailing, banking on circuit attrition rather than burning matches for minor places—paid off with third GC.
EF Education's Higuita and Carapaz repeatedly attacked for KOM points across laps, indicating breakaway riders should prioritize jersey competitions when stage win unlikely on circuits.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: forced to self-isolate and attack at 26km after Quick-Step's early pacing destroyed his own support, revealing team strategy backfired
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: matched every Evenepoel acceleration on circuits while Bora refused chase duties, showing superior team discipline and individual strength
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: UAE's strongest GC rider finished fourth ahead of Ayuso, suggesting Pogacar's absence elevated wrong team leader internally
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: prioritized KOM jersey from breakaway rather than conserving for GC support, indicating EF shifted goals mid-race
